title: persona-distill manifest.json contract
description: The manifest.json produced by distill-meta for every generated persona skill. Consumed by persona-judge (routing of rubric dimensions), persona-router (matching), persona-debate (participant discovery), and the migrator agent (component-version drift detection and user-edit detection). This schema is the ONLY authoritative contract for that file; changes here are breaking changes to all 5 skills.
$comment: Version 0.2.0 — (a) schema_type relaxed from a fixed enum of 9 values to a slug pattern to accommodate community-contributed schemas per contracts/schema-extension-contract.md (origin and author fields added); (b) migration support added per references/migration.md — distill_meta_version, migration_history[], last_migrated_at, components_fingerprint, and components_used widened via oneOf to optionally carry per-slug version + original_component_hash for drift and user-edit detection. Core schemas: self|collaborator|mentor|loved-one|friend|public-mirror|public-domain|topic|executor.

persona_distill_version.description: SemVer of the persona-distill plugin that generated this manifest.

distill_meta_version.description: v0.2.0+. SemVer of the distill-meta skill (or persona-distill plugin) that last wrote this manifest — which may be a generator pass (Phase 5) OR a migrator pass (Stage 3c). Distinct from persona_distill_version, which is plugin-level; this field is bumped by the migrator so drift detection can tell whether the on-disk components match the current library without re-reading every component file. See references/migration.md §Manifest Fields Added in v0.2.0.

schema_type.description: Slug of the persona schema this skill uses. Core schemas: self | collaborator | mentor | loved-one | friend | public-mirror | public-domain | topic | executor. Community schemas accepted per schema-extension-contract.md (MUST be validated against that contract before the producing distill-meta run accepts them).

schema_type_origin.description: Whether schema_type refers to one of the 9 built-in core schemas or a community-contributed schema. Downstream tools may surface this distinction (e.g., persona-router de-ranking; persona-judge stricter threshold for unvalidated community schemas).

schema_type_author.description: Author/handle of the community schema's contributor, copied from the schema file's frontmatter. Optional for core schemas; REQUIRED when schema_type_origin = community.

schema_version.description: Version of the schema definition used. Allows future migration.

components_used.description: Slugs of the 18 shared components activated for this persona. v0.2.0 widens this to optionally carry per-slug version + original_component_hash so the migrator can detect component-library drift and user hand-edits. Both shapes validate (oneOf); new generators SHOULD emit the object form. Pre-v0.2.0 manifests using the legacy string form remain valid; the migrator treats them as third-party / no-known-baseline skills (diff-only mode, no auto-patch). See references/migration.md §Failure Modes.

oneOf.description: Legacy form (pre-v0.2.0). Array of slug strings.

v0.2.0+ form. Array of objects per slug, carrying the component definition version resolved at generation time and the SHA-256 of the generated file content at generation/migration time (the baseline the migrator compares against for user-edit detection).

version.description: SemVer of the component definition in distill-meta/references/components/<slug>.md at the time this persona's copy was written or last migrated.

original_component_hash.description: SHA-256 (64-hex lowercase) of the persona's components/<slug>.md content at the time it was last written by distill-meta or the migrator. Used as the baseline for user-edit detection: if the on-disk hash no longer matches this value, the file has been hand-edited and the migrator will refuse auto-patch (forcing diff review). Null is allowed for third-party or pre-v0.2.0 skills with no known baseline.

identity.description: Identity card. Used by persona-router for matching.

description.description: One-paragraph summary. Router matches against this.

domains.description: Domains / areas of expertise the persona is suited for.

subject_type.description: Whether this is a real person, composite, fictional, or rule system.

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 over the concatenated, sorted list of knowledge/*.md file contents. Stable across rebuilds when knowledge is unchanged. This field is NOT recomputed during migration — migration only touches components/, never knowledge/. Recomputing it during migration would mask genuine knowledge edits made between migration runs.

components_fingerprint.description: v0.2.0+. SHA-256 (64-hex lowercase) over the sorted concatenation of components/**/*.md content hashes, separated by \n. Written by distill-meta at generation and by the migrator whenever any component is rewritten. Distinct from fingerprint (which hashes knowledge/); this field lets consumers tell whether the persona's component set matches a given distill-meta library state without reading every component file.

version.description: SemVer of this persona skill itself. Bumped on knowledge updates or corrections.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this manifest was first written by distill-meta.

last_migrated_at.description: v0.2.0+. ISO 8601 timestamp of the last successful migration pass, or null on first generation (no migrations yet). Set by the migrator at step 6 of the 7-step procedure (references/migration.md §Migration Procedure).

migration_history.description: v0.2.0+. Append-only ledger of migration runs. Empty array on first generation. Each entry records the from/to distill-meta versions, which components were actually rewritten, and whether the user approved the result (false entries represent halted or rolled-back attempts — the migrator still records them for auditability). See references/migration.md.

migrated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of this migration pass.

components_changed.description: Slugs of components actually rewritten in this pass. Empty array means only manifest metadata was touched.

user_approved.description: True if the user approved the committed result; false if the pass was halted (regression > threshold) or rolled back.

validation_score_at_migration.description: persona-judge raw score (0-110) captured at step 7 of this pass. Null if the pass did not reach step 7 (e.g., halted earlier).

density_score.description: Most recent density score from persona-judge. 0-10. -1 if not yet evaluated.

validation_score.description: Most recent validation score from persona-judge. 0-110 raw, -1 if not evaluated, or null immediately after a migration pass (step 6 sets null to force re-validation at step 7 of references/migration.md).

unvalidated.description: True for skills shipped before dog-food validation. Consumers may warn users.

primary_ratio.description: Fraction of corpus from primary sources (first-hand).

allOf.$comment: When schema_type_origin is community, schema_type_author is required (per schema-extension-contract.md §6).
